name: Everolimus Aging Study is active with no posted results
summary: The Everolimus Aging Study is direct Rapalogs coverage for active human geroprotection testing, but remains no-results registry context until the metabolic, cardiac, cognitive, molecular, and safety outcomes are reported.
statement: ClinicalTrials.gov lists NCT05835999 as an active-not-recruiting 106-participant phase 2 trial of daily or weekly everolimus in older insulin-resistant or prediabetic adults, with insulin-sensitivity, cardiac, cognitive, molecular, and safety endpoints and no posted results in the June 11, 2026 API view.
population_or_model: Adults aged 55 to 80 years with insulin resistance or prediabetes
time_horizon: Twenty-four-week intervention with safety follow-up up to 36 weeks
quantitative_note: Actual enrollment is 106; estimated primary and study completion are both September 2026; results were not posted.
caveats: No-results registry status is not evidence of benefit or harm.
The population is insulin-resistant or prediabetic rather than general healthy aging.
Safety and metabolic readouts will be central because everolimus can have dose- and schedule-sensitive metabolic and immune effects.
